report_name: FBI and Google Collaborate to Disrupt 'NetNut' Residential Proxy Botnet

attack_patterns.name: External Proxy
attack_patterns.description: The NetNut service operated as a commercial residential proxy network, allowing cybercriminals to route their traffic through millions of compromised devices to hide their true origins while conducting activities like phishing, credential stuffing, and anonymizing C2 traffic.

attack_patterns.name: Credential Stuffing
attack_patterns.description: The NetNut residential proxy botnet was used by cybercriminals to launch large-scale credential stuffing attacks, appearing to come from millions of different legitimate IPs to avoid detection and blocking.

attack_patterns.name: Phishing
attack_patterns.description: The NetNut botnet infrastructure was used by threat actors to hide the true location of their phishing servers.

malware.name: NetNut
malware.description: A residential proxy botnet comprised of millions of infected devices globally. The malware infected devices, turning them into proxies that were rented out to cybercriminals for anonymizing malicious activities.

identities.name: Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI)
identities.description: Collaborated with Google to disrupt the NetNut residential proxy botnet through technical and legal actions, including sinkholing C2 traffic and seizing infrastructure.

identities.name: Google
identities.description: Collaborated with the FBI to disrupt the NetNut residential proxy botnet, contributing to malware analysis, victim notification, and pushing updates to protect users.

infrastructure.name: NetNut Residential Proxy Botnet
infrastructure.description: A massive network of millions of compromised residential devices rented out to cybercriminals. Its command-and-control infrastructure was disrupted and sinkholed by the FBI and Google.
